title: Trachynotia cynosuroides
intro: Trachynotia cynosuroides, commonly known as the Devil's Claw or Grapple Plant, is a flowering plant species in the family Apocynaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering relief from pain, inflammation, and digestive issues due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Trachynoides cynosuroides include harpagoside, a unique iridoid glycoside, as well as other phenolic compounds, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include capsules, tablets, and teas, often used to treat conditions such as arthritis, rheumatism, and other inflammatory diseases.
